items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical
items.summary: Amasty released a significant security update for 25 Magento extensions, addressing at least one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload vulnerability in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without authentication, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer allows remote code execution via a crafted cookie, affecting all versions prior to 1.11.12. CISA has added this actively exploited flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical flaw in Magento's REST API, dubbed 'PolyShell', allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by using a polyglot technique to bypass validation. Sansec has observed attacks exploiting this vulnerability on a significant percentage of protected stores.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could allow attackers to bypass security mechanisms, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code.

items.title: SessionReaper, unauthenticated RCE in Magento & Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical vulnerability, named 'SessionReaper', allows unauthenticated remote code execution and customer account takeover in Magento and Adobe Commerce. Despite an emergency fix in September 2025, automated attacks exploiting this flaw have been observed globally.
